channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: ASEFYLS 2026 Capacity Training: Fully Funded Youth Leadership Program (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Are you a young leader eager to shape the future and create meaningful change in your community? The ASEF Young Leaders Summit (ASEFYLS) Capacity Training 2026 is now accepting applications for its latest cohort. This dynamic programme empowers youth from Asia and Europe to develop leadership skills, explore future-thinking strategies, and drive real-world impact. What […]

The post ASEFYLS 2026 Capacity Training: Fully Funded Youth Leadership Program (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a young leader eager to shape the future and create meaningful change in your community? The ASEF Young Leaders Summit (ASEFYLS) Capacity Training 2026 is now accepting applications for its latest cohort. This dynamic programme empowers youth from Asia and Europe to develop leadership skills, explore future-thinking strategies, and drive real-world impact.

What is ASEFYLS?

The ASEF Young Leaders Summit (ASEFYLS) is a youth platform designed to build a strong network of young leaders across Asia and Europe. It offers a transformative learning journey focused on:

  • Self-leadership (You)
  • Team leadership (We)
  • Societal leadership (All)

The programme fosters curiosity, innovation, and intercultural understanding—equipping participants with the tools to become forward-thinking leaders who can drive sustainable and inclusive change.

At the heart of ASEFYLS 2026 is the concept of Anticipatory Leadership—the ability to use insights about the future to inform present decisions. This includes analyzing trends, identifying emerging signals, and adapting leadership approaches to build more equitable and sustainable societies.

Programme Theme: Anticipatory Leadership for a Human-Centred Future

The 2026 edition will focus on “Anticipatory Leadership for a Human-Centred Future.” Participants will explore how to:

  • Analyze trends and weak signals
  • Apply strategic foresight and systems thinking
  • Understand the ethical implications of emerging technologies like AI
  • Lead with inclusivity and sustainability in mind

Why You Should Apply

Learn

Gain insights from leading institutions, thematic experts, and high-level speakers on future-focused leadership.

Network

Connect with like-minded young leaders from across Asia and Europe and build long-term partnerships.

Take Action

Apply your knowledge in your local community through impactful grassroots initiatives.

What to Expect in ASEFYLS 2026

The programme runs from June to November 2026 and includes the following phases:

1. Training Phase (Online)

9 June – 30 July 2026

  • Participate in interactive online sessions (approx. 4 hours per week)
  • Learn about anticipatory leadership, systems thinking, and foresight tools
  • Explore how technology influences ethical and inclusive leadership

2. Action Phase (Local Implementation)

August – November 2026

  • Organize multiplier events in your local community
  • Share knowledge and drive impact at the grassroots level
  • Translate theory into real-world action

3. Optional In-Person Training (Selected Participants)

Asia-Europe Youth Leadership Training: Anticipatory Leadership in the Arctic
 Tromsø, Norway
23 – 29 August 2026

  • 40 participants will be selected based on engagement
  • Dive deeper into anticipatory leadership using the Arctic as a case study
  • Engage in cross-cultural exchange and expert dialogues

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of eligible Asian or European countries
  • Be 18–30 years old (students or young professionals)
  • Be actively involved in youth or student organizations
  • Demonstrate leadership and community engagement
  • Have strong English communication skills

Selection will be based on motivation, leadership experience, and potential to create impact in local communities.

Programme Benefits

  • No registration or participation fees
  • Travel subsidies (for selected participants)
  •  Accommodation and meals covered during in-person training
  •  Access to a global network of young leaders
  • Hands-on leadership and future-thinking training

Commitment Required

Participants are expected to:

  • Attend 2 online sessions per week during the training phase
  • Complete self-paced assignments
  • Organize community-based multiplier events
  • (If selected) Participate in the in-person training in Norway

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official programme portal.

APPLY HERE!

Deadline 12 May 2026!

Pro Tip

Highlight your community impact and leadership experience in your application. Showcase how you plan to apply anticipatory leadership in solving real challenges in your local context.

Register Here, For FAQ Webinar

ASEFYLS 2026 is more than just a training programme—it’s a platform to reimagine the future and actively shape it. If you’re passionate about leadership, innovation, and making a difference, this is your opportunity to join a global movement of changemakers.

Discover similar opportunities  here.

The post ASEFYLS 2026 Capacity Training: Fully Funded Youth Leadership Program (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Democracy Camp 2026 Germany: A Transformative Youth Program on Democracy, Sustainability, and European Participation
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Democracy Camp 2026 in Germany is a five-day international youth exchange program designed to empower young people to actively engage with democracy, sustainability, intersectionality, and European cooperation. Taking place in Vallendar, Germany, this immersive experience brings together 60 young participants from across Europe to explore how democratic systems function at different levels and how […]

The post Democracy Camp 2026 Germany: A Transformative Youth Program on Democracy, Sustainability, and European Participation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Democracy Camp 2026 in Germany is a five-day international youth exchange program designed to empower young people to actively engage with democracy, sustainability, intersectionality, and European cooperation. Taking place in Vallendar, Germany, this immersive experience brings together 60 young participants from across Europe to explore how democratic systems function at different levels and how youth can meaningfully influence political and social change.

This program is not just a learning experience it is a collaborative space where participants co-create ideas, develop civic engagement skills, and contribute to a tangible output: the Youth Participation Handbook, a policy-oriented publication aimed at improving youth inclusion in democratic processes across Europe.

Overview of Democracy Camp 2026

Democracy Camp 2026 is structured as an intercultural training and dialogue platform that connects young people aged 16 to 26 from multiple European countries. The camp focuses on bridging the gap between youth and decision-making institutions while strengthening understanding of democracy in practice.

Participants will engage in interactive workshops, group discussions, field visits, and collaborative projects. The program is built around real-world challenges such as climate change, social inequality, inclusion, and democratic participation at local, national, and European levels.

The event will take place from 17 June to 21 June 2026 in Haus der Familie, Vallendar, Germany, with a digital preparatory session held in May 2026.

Core Themes and Learning Objectives

The Democracy Camp 2026 is built around two central guiding questions:

  1. What participation tools are available for young people today?
  2. How can these tools be used to promote sustainability, inclusion, and democratic engagement?

Through these questions, participants will explore key thematic areas:

Democracy and Political Participation

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of democratic systems, from local governance to European Union structures and global institutions such as the United Nations. The camp emphasizes practical participation tools, including advocacy, civic campaigns, and youth-led initiatives.

Sustainability and Climate Action

The program highlights the relationship between democracy and sustainability, encouraging participants to examine how political decisions affect environmental outcomes and how youth can influence climate policy.

Intersectionality and Inclusion

A major focus is placed on social justice, equality, and inclusion. Participants will explore how intersecting identities influence access to political participation and how inclusive democracies can be strengthened.

Youth Empowerment and Leadership

The camp develops leadership, communication, and project management skills, enabling participants to design and implement impactful civic initiatives in their home countries.

Program Structure and Activities

The Democracy Camp 2026 is carefully structured into three phases:

1. Digital Kick-Off Session (26 May 2026)

Participants will meet online before the camp begins to share ideas, set expectations, and connect with facilitators and fellow participants. This session introduces key themes and allows participants to shape the agenda.

2. In-Person Camp in Vallendar (17–21 June 2026)

The main camp includes intensive workshops, intercultural exchange activities, and collaborative sessions. Participants will explore democratic practices across Europe, analyze case studies, and develop practical participation tools.

A key highlight includes a study visit to Bonn, where participants will engage with the United Nations Campus and meet local civic initiatives working on democratic participation and sustainability.

Evenings will be dedicated to reflection, networking, and intercultural exchange activities, culminating in a final celebration event.

3. Post-Camp Publication and Impact Phase

After the camp, participants will co-author the Youth Participation Handbook 2026. This publication will include recommendations, tools, and insights designed to improve youth participation across Europe. The handbook will be shared with policymakers, including members of the European Parliament, to influence future youth engagement strategies.

Costs and Financial Support

One of the most attractive aspects of Democracy Camp 2026 is its full financial coverage:

  • Participation is completely free of charge
  • Travel reimbursement up to 250 EUR is provided
  • Accommodation and meals are fully covered
  • Vegetarian and vegan meals are provided throughout the program

Participants are encouraged to use environmentally friendly travel options such as trains, buses, or carpooling, which are prioritized in the selection process.

Accommodation and Facilities

The camp will be hosted at Haus der Familie in Vallendar, Germany. The venue offers a comfortable and interactive environment with facilities such as sports fields, recreational spaces, and group activity areas. These facilities are designed to support both structured learning and informal intercultural exchange.

Who Can Apply

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Age between 16 and 26 years
  • Ability to communicate in English for the duration of the program
  • Willingness to engage in intercultural dialogue and teamwork
  • Interest in democracy, sustainability, and civic participation

Applications from young people with fewer opportunities are strongly encouraged and prioritized to ensure diversity and inclusion.

Minors are welcome but must provide parental consent and be accompanied according to program safety guidelines.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for Democracy Camp 2026 is 16 March 2026.

Why You Should Apply

Democracy Camp 2026 offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Connect with young leaders from across Europe
  • Develop real-world civic engagement skills
  • Understand European and global democratic systems
  • Contribute to a policy-relevant publication
  • Build a network of changemakers
  • Gain hands-on experience in international collaboration

This program is ideal for young people who are passionate about democracy, climate action, social justice, and international cooperation.

Final Thoughts

Democracy Camp 2026 is more than a youth exchange it is a platform for shaping the future of democratic participation in Europe. By bringing together diverse voices and ideas, the program empowers young people to become active contributors to policy discussions and social transformation.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities click here 

The post Democracy Camp 2026 Germany: A Transformative Youth Program on Democracy, Sustainability, and European Participation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Careers at the Center for Global Development (CGD): Jobs, Internships & Fellowships to Tackle Global Poverty
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
If you are passionate about solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges—poverty, inequality, and sustainable development—then a career at the Center for Global Development (CGD) could be the opportunity you’ve been looking for. CGD is a globally recognized think tank dedicated to producing high-impact research and policy solutions that improve lives in developing countries. […]

The post Careers at the Center for Global Development (CGD): Jobs, Internships & Fellowships to Tackle Global Poverty appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges—poverty, inequality, and sustainable development—then a career at the Center for Global Development (CGD) could be the opportunity you’ve been looking for.

CGD is a globally recognized think tank dedicated to producing high-impact research and policy solutions that improve lives in developing countries. Through its careers, internships, and fellowships programs, CGD offers professionals and early-career talents the chance to work at the intersection of research, policy, and global impact.

Why Work at the Center for Global Development?

At CGD, people are at the heart of everything. The organization places strong emphasis on:

  • Intellectual excellence and innovation
  • Diversity, equity, and inclusion
  • Collaboration and professional growth
  • Real-world policy impact

Employees are not just part of a workplace—they are part of a mission-driven community working to reduce global poverty and inequality through evidence-based solutions.

Current Job and Internship Opportunities

CGD regularly recruits talented individuals across different levels and areas of expertise. Current openings include:

1. Institutional Advancement Internship – Washington, DC

An excellent entry point for students or recent graduates interested in:

  • Development fundraising and partnerships
  • Nonprofit operations
  • Communications and donor engagement

APPLY HERE 

2. Research Assistant – Gender Equality (Temporary) – Washington, DC

A role suited for candidates with strong analytical skills and interest in:

  • Gender equality and social policy
  • Development research
  • Data analysis and policy evaluation

APPLY HERE 

📌 Important Note: Due to the high volume of applications, CGD only contacts shortlisted candidates for interviews.

Life at CGD: A Dynamic and Inclusive Work Environment

CGD is known for fostering a workplace culture that is:

  • Collaborative and collegial
  • Intellectually stimulating
  • Supportive of innovation and growth

Employees benefit from a vibrant environment that combines serious policy work with opportunities for social interaction and well-being.

Benefits for U.S.-Based Employees

Full-time employees in the United States enjoy a highly competitive benefits package, including:

  • Comprehensive health and dental insurance
  • 6 weeks of paid time off (PTO) annually
  • 15 paid holidays
  • Employer-paid life and disability insurance
  • Generous parental leave
  • Retirement contribution (15%) after one year
  • Onsite fitness center and wellness programs
  • Tuition reimbursement opportunities
  • Social activities such as sports leagues, events, and team gatherings

Benefits for CGD Europe (UK-Based Staff)

For employees based in CGD Europe, benefits include:

  • 25 days annual leave + bank holidays
  • Enhanced pension contributions
  • Family and parental support benefits
  • Health and insurance coverage
  • Learning and development funding
  • Flexible work arrangements (minimum 3 days in office)
  • Cycle-to-work scheme
  • Staff support networks and wellbeing initiatives

Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

CGD is deeply committed to building an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected.

All applicants are considered without discrimination based on:

  • Race or ethnicity
  • Gender identity or expression
  • Sexual orientation
  • Religion or disability
  • Age or veteran status

This commitment ensures that CGD continues to benefit from diverse perspectives and experiences, which are essential to solving global challenges.

Career Growth and Learning Opportunities

Working at CGD is not just about a job—it’s about continuous growth.

Employees have access to:

  • Professional development programs
  • Research collaborations with global experts
  • Opportunities to contribute to influential policy debates
  • Mentorship and career advancement pathways

Why Choose CGD?

Joining CGD means becoming part of a global effort to:

  • Reduce poverty
  • Promote equality
  • Influence international development policy
  • Create long-term, sustainable change

Whether you are starting your career or looking to advance in the development sector, CGD offers a platform to make meaningful impact at a global scale.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Careers at the Center for Global Development (CGD): Jobs, Internships & Fellowships to Tackle Global Poverty appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New American Speakers Program 2026 (Cohort 10): Fully Funded Storytelling & Public Speaking Training for Refugees in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Refugee Storytellers Collective is now accepting applications for the New American Speakers Program (NASP) – Cohort 10, a highly impactful initiative designed to empower refugees and immigrants in the United States to reclaim their narratives, amplify their voices, and become influential storytellers and advocates. In a world where migration narratives are often shaped by […]

The post New American Speakers Program 2026 (Cohort 10): Fully Funded Storytelling & Public Speaking Training for Refugees in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Refugee Storytellers Collective is now accepting applications for the New American Speakers Program (NASP) – Cohort 10, a highly impactful initiative designed to empower refugees and immigrants in the United States to reclaim their narratives, amplify their voices, and become influential storytellers and advocates.

In a world where migration narratives are often shaped by misinformation or oversimplification, this program offers something powerful: the opportunity for those with lived experience to tell their own stories—on their own terms.

Why This Program Matters More Than Ever

Refugees and immigrants are frequently spoken about—but rarely given the platform to speak for themselves. Misconceptions, stereotypes, and political narratives often dominate public discourse.

The New American Speakers Program directly challenges this by:

  • Centering authentic lived experiences
  • Training participants to become confident public speakers
  • Equipping them to influence public opinion and policy conversations
  • Building a network of storytellers who advocate for more inclusive and accurate narratives

This is not just a training—it is a movement toward narrative justice and representation.

A Deep Dive into the Program Experience

The NASP Cohort 10 is a 10-week immersive journey (May 11 – July 24, 2026), delivered entirely online to ensure accessibility for participants across the United States.

What You Will Learn

Participants go far beyond basic public speaking. The program is carefully designed to develop a wide range of skills:

🎤 1. Public Speaking Mastery

  • Overcoming fear and building confidence
  • Structuring and delivering impactful speeches
  • Engaging diverse audiences

✍️ 2. Storytelling & Narrative Building

  • Transforming personal experiences into compelling narratives
  • Identifying key themes and messages
  • Balancing emotion, clarity, and purpose

🌍 3. Advocacy & Leadership

  • Using storytelling as a tool for social change
  • Speaking in policy, academic, and community spaces
  • Representing refugee and immigrant voices responsibly

🤝 4. Collaboration & Community Building

  • Learning alongside peers with shared experiences
  • Giving and receiving feedback in a safe environment
  • Building long-term connections and support systems

A Safe and Empowering Learning Environment

One of the most unique aspects of this program is its intentional design as a safe and inclusive space.

Participants are:

  • Surrounded by individuals with shared lived experiences of displacement
  • Encouraged to express themselves without fear of judgment
  • Supported by facilitators who understand the emotional and social complexities of storytelling

This environment is essential for enabling participants to open up, reflect deeply, and grow confidently.

Storytelling Nights: Where Learning Meets Real Impact

A standout feature of the program is the Storytelling Nights—live events where participants present their stories to public audiences.

📅 Tentative Dates:

  • June 17, 2026
  • July 23, 2026

These sessions are more than practice—they are:

  • Real opportunities to engage with audiences
  • Platforms to challenge stereotypes and inspire change
  • Moments where participants step into their role as public advocates and speakers

What Happens After the Program?

Graduating from NASP is not the end—it is just the beginning.

Successful participants will:

  • Receive a certificate of completion
  • Become official members of the Refugee Storytellers Collective
  • Gain access to an exclusive network of speaking opportunities
  • Be connected with event organizers, institutions, and platforms seeking speakers

This means your story doesn’t just stay in the classroom—it reaches the world.

Financial Accessibility: A Fully Funded Opportunity

All selected participants may receive a scholarship covering the full cost of the program, making this opportunity accessible regardless of financial background.

This ensures that talent, voice, and potential—not financial means—determine participation.

Who Should Apply?

This program is ideal for individuals who:

  • Have experienced forced displacement (refugees, asylum seekers, etc.)
  • Are currently based in the United States
  • Want to improve their public speaking and storytelling skills
  • Are passionate about advocacy, representation, and social impact

Priority Consideration

Special attention will be given to applicants who are:

  • Veterans
  • Business owners
  • Faith leaders

These individuals often hold influential roles in their communities and can amplify impact even further.

Application Process: How to Stand Out

The application process is designed to understand your story, motivation, and potential.

Steps:

  1. Complete the online application form
  2. Provide detailed responses about your experiences and goals
  3. Participate in a Zoom interview if shortlisted

Tips for a Strong Application:

  • Be authentic and honest—your lived experience is your strength
  • Clearly explain why storytelling matters to you
  • Show your willingness to engage, learn, and grow
  • Highlight any past experiences in communication, leadership, or advocacy (if applicable)

Important Dates to Remember

  • 📅 Application Deadline: April 17, 2026
  • 🚀 Program Start Date: May 11, 2026
  • 🎤 Program End Date: July 24, 2026

The Bigger Impact: Changing Narratives, One Story at a Time

The New American Speakers Program is not just about individual growth—it contributes to a broader mission:

  • Shifting public narratives around migration
  • Promoting empathy and understanding
  • Building bridges between communities
  • Empowering underrepresented voices

Every story shared has the potential to educate, inspire, and transform perspectives.

Final Thoughts

If you have ever felt that your story deserves to be heard—but didn’t know how to share it—this program is your opportunity.

The New American Speakers Program offers more than skills. It offers:

  • A platform
  • A community
  • A voice

And most importantly, it offers the chance to turn your personal journey into a force for change.

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post New American Speakers Program 2026 (Cohort 10): Fully Funded Storytelling & Public Speaking Training for Refugees in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager Job in Nairobi (2026): Work with Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) is currently hiring an Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager based in Nairobi, Kenya. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity for professionals passionate about higher education access, refugee inclusion, and global development. Hosted at the British Institute in Eastern Africa (BIEA), this role plays a critical part in advancing RLRH’s […]

The post Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager Job in Nairobi (2026): Work with Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) is currently hiring an Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager based in Nairobi, Kenya. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity for professionals passionate about higher education access, refugee inclusion, and global development.

Hosted at the British Institute in Eastern Africa (BIEA), this role plays a critical part in advancing RLRH’s ambitious goal of supporting 1,000+ displacement-affected students to access postgraduate education worldwide by 2028.

Job Overview

  • Position: Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya (Kileleshwa, with occasional travel)
  • Salary: KSh 3,234,111 – 3,727,222 annually (gross)
  • Contract Type: 12-month fixed-term (extendable until at least October 2028)
  • Working Hours: Full-time (37.5 hours/week)
  • Start Date: 1 June 2026 (flexible)
  • Application Deadline: 4 May 2026 at 5:00 PM EAT

About the Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH)

RLRH is a global initiative supporting individuals with lived experience of displacement to become leaders in:

  • Human rights
  • Forced migration
  • International development
  • Law and policy

With a network of over 1,400 members across 80+ countries, RLRH operates in multiple locations including Nairobi, Oxford, Kuala Lumpur, Kakuma Refugee Camp, and Kampala.

Through partnerships like the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program, RLRH helps displacement-affected students access graduate-level education and career opportunities worldwide.

About the Role

This role is central to RLRH’s expansion strategy and focuses on:

  • Building partnerships with 30+ universities globally
  • Leading outreach campaigns to recruit displacement-affected students
  • Advocating for inclusive admissions systems
  • Driving data-informed recruitment and engagement strategies

The position combines strategy, partnerships, advocacy, and operations, making it ideal for professionals with experience in international education, NGOs, or development sectors.

Key Responsibilities

1. Partnership Development (35%)

  • Build and manage relationships with global universities and scholarship programs
  • Collaborate with partners like UNHCR and education-focused organizations
  • Develop engagement strategies and track partnership outcomes

2. Strategic Growth & Advocacy (25%)

  • Identify barriers in admissions systems
  • Promote inclusive recruitment practices
  • Advocate for policy changes in higher education access

3. Student & Mentor Recruitment (20%)

  • Lead large-scale outreach campaigns
  • Engage 1,000+ students and 200+ mentors annually
  • Organize info sessions and recruitment events

4. Data Management & Reporting (20%)

  • Track admissions and recruitment data
  • Analyze trends and improve strategies
  • Produce reports for stakeholders and donors

Eligibility Criteria

Essential Requirements:

  • Minimum 5+ years experience in partnerships, programs, or higher education
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership skills
  • Experience with data analysis, reporting, and recruitment strategies
  • Knowledge of inclusive education and refugee access challenges
  • Proficiency in digital outreach tools (e.g., Canva, mailing platforms)

Desirable:

  • Experience with the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
  • Background in migration, education, or development studies
  • Additional language skills
  • Postgraduate degree (or equivalent experience)

Inclusivity & Work Eligibility

  • Applicants must have (or be able to obtain) the right to work in Kenya
  • RLRH supports displaced applicants with obtaining a Refugee Class M work permit
  • Strong encouragement for applications from:
    • Refugees and displaced individuals
    • Women, LGBTQI+, disabled persons
    • Underrepresented communities

Application Process

Applicants must submit:

  • CV (max 3 pages)
  • Cover letter
  • Responses to 3 short questions (250 words each)
  • 1–2 writing samples (max 2,000 words each)
  • Two professional references

📌 Important: Use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) is not allowed in application materials.

Why You Should Apply

This role offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Work at the intersection of education, migration, and global policy
  • Influence access to higher education for refugees worldwide
  • Collaborate with leading universities and global organizations
  • Be part of a mission-driven, diverse, and international team

If you are passionate about equity in education and systemic change, this role is a powerful platform to make a global impact.

For more information click  here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here

The post Academic Partnerships & Outreach Manager Job in Nairobi (2026): Work with Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CRE Call for Proposals 2026 – Africa: Grants to Support Community-Led Human Rights Initiatives in Critical Minerals
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Community Resource Exchange (CRE) has officially launched its 2026 Call for Proposals for Africa, offering funding to support community-driven initiatives that defend human rights in the context of international investments and development projects. This funding opportunity is particularly focused on critical minerals, a rapidly growing sector linked to global energy transitions and industrial development. […]

The post CRE Call for Proposals 2026 – Africa: Grants to Support Community-Led Human Rights Initiatives in Critical Minerals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Community Resource Exchange (CRE) has officially launched its 2026 Call for Proposals for Africa, offering funding to support community-driven initiatives that defend human rights in the context of international investments and development projects.

This funding opportunity is particularly focused on critical minerals, a rapidly growing sector linked to global energy transitions and industrial development. Communities affected by projects involving minerals such as lithium, cobalt, copper, and rare earth elements are encouraged to apply for support to strengthen their advocacy and protection efforts.

About the CRE Collaboration Grants

The CRE Collaboration Grants are designed to empower communities and grassroots actors by funding collaborative projects that address the challenges posed by large-scale investments and development initiatives.

Rather than working in isolation, CRE promotes collective strategy-building, enabling communities to partner with researchers, advocates, and organizations to create meaningful and sustainable impact.

Grant Details

  • 💰 Maximum Grant Amount: USD $5,000
  • ⏳ Project Duration: Up to 8 months
  • 🌍 Region: Africa
  • 📅 Application Deadline: 7 May 2026
  • 📢 Decision Announcement: August 2026

This flexible funding is ideal for small to medium-scale projects that require targeted support to address urgent community needs.

Thematic Focus: Critical Minerals

This year’s call focuses specifically on critical minerals, which are essential for:

  • Renewable energy technologies
  • Electric vehicles
  • Digital infrastructure
  • National and global economic development

Eligible projects may involve activities connected to:

  • Multilateral development banks
  • National development banks
  • Development agencies
  • Export credit agencies
  • International companies and investors

📌 Important: Applicants do NOT need to prove a confirmed link to these actors—a potential or likely connection is sufficient.

Types of Activities Supported

CRE encourages proposals that include collaborative and strategic actions such as:

  • Advocacy with policymakers and decision-makers
  • Media engagement and public campaigns
  • Community organizing and mobilization
  • Corporate and financial research
  • Scientific and technical impact analysis
  • Filing complaints and seeking remedies
  • Security support for affected communities
  • Resource mobilization efforts

These activities aim to strengthen the capacity of communities to defend their rights and influence decision-making processes.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered, proposals must:

  • Be fully completed using the official application form
  • Be community-driven and collaborative
  • Focus on defending human rights in the context of development or investment projects
  • Align with the critical minerals theme
  • Demonstrate a clear link (actual or potential) to international investments

Incomplete applications will not be reviewed, so attention to detail is essential.

Important Notes

  • CRE may contact applicants for clarification after submission
  • Funding is transferred via international bank transfer only
  • Applicants without bank accounts must partner with an organization that can receive funds on their behalf
  • Due to high volume, only selected applicants will be contacted

About the CRE

The Community Resource Exchange is a collaborative system that supports communities defending their rights in the face of large-scale development and investment projects.

Currently in its second phase, CRE focuses on:

  • Evidence-building
  • Learning and knowledge sharing
  • Strengthening grassroots collaboration
  • Supporting community-led solutions

This approach ensures that communities are not just beneficiaries, but active agents of change.

Why You Should Apply

This grant is a powerful opportunity to:

  • Strengthen community advocacy and protection efforts
  • Access funding for grassroots initiatives
  • Collaborate with experts and organizations
  • Influence development practices and accountability
  • Address the impacts of critical mineral extraction

If your community is facing challenges linked to development projects or resource extraction, this grant can help you take action.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post CRE Call for Proposals 2026 – Africa: Grants to Support Community-Led Human Rights Initiatives in Critical Minerals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Research on Regular & Irregular Migration with Danish Refugee Council (DRC)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Danish Refugee Council (DRC) is offering an exciting international consultancy opportunity for experts in migration, policy, and humanitarian research. Under a grant funded by Danida, this call invites qualified professionals and organizations to submit proposals for a research project exploring the interaction between regular and irregular migration. With global migration becoming increasingly complex, this […]

The post Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Research on Regular & Irregular Migration with Danish Refugee Council (DRC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Danish Refugee Council (DRC) is offering an exciting international consultancy opportunity for experts in migration, policy, and humanitarian research. Under a grant funded by Danida, this call invites qualified professionals and organizations to submit proposals for a research project exploring the interaction between regular and irregular migration.

With global migration becoming increasingly complex, this consultancy represents a unique chance to contribute to high-impact research that informs humanitarian strategies and policy decisions worldwide.

About the Consultancy

This consultancy is part of DRC’s broader humanitarian operations and aims to generate in-depth research on how regular (legal) and irregular (unauthorized) migration pathways intersect, influence, and shape each other.

The selected consultant or team will be responsible for:

  • Conducting rigorous, evidence-based research
  • Analyzing migration patterns and policy frameworks
  • Delivering actionable insights to inform humanitarian programming
  • Producing high-quality reports aligned with DRC standards

This work will contribute directly to improving global understanding of migration dynamics and supporting more effective responses for displaced populations.

Key Details

  • Reference Number: RFP-DKHQ-002662
  • Organization: Danish Refugee Council (DRC)
  • Location: HQ | Geneva
  • Contract Type: One-time consultancy (service contract)
  • Category: Request for Proposal (RFP) – Services
  • Proposal Language: English
  • Proposal Validity: Minimum 90 days

About the Danish Refugee Council (DRC)

Founded in 1956, the Danish Refugee Council is Denmark’s largest humanitarian NGO and a globally recognized leader in displacement response.

Operating in nearly 30 countries, DRC works to:

  • Protect displaced populations
  • Advocate for refugee rights
  • Support sustainable and dignified livelihoods

With over 6,000 staff and thousands of volunteers, DRC continues to play a critical role in addressing one of the world’s most pressing challenges—forced displacement.

Scope of Work

The consultancy focuses on a critical and timely research theme:

Interaction Between Regular & Irregular Migration

Key research areas may include:

  • The relationship between legal migration systems and irregular migration flows
  • Policy gaps and unintended consequences of migration regulations
  • Socio-economic and political drivers influencing migration pathways
  • Implications for humanitarian programming and migration governance

Applicants are expected to carefully review the Terms of Reference (TOR – Annex F) for detailed deliverables and expectations.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit a formal proposal in response to the Request for Proposal (RFP).

Requirements:

  • Proposal must be written in English
  • Must remain valid for at least 90 days
  • Must follow the guidelines outlined in the RFP and TOR documents

Submission:

Applicants are required to:

  • Download the official documents
  • Prepare a comprehensive technical and financial proposal
  • Submit via the official DRC procurement platform or instructed channel

Important Deadline

  • Published: 09 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 23 April 2026 at 08:00 UTC

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to ensure compliance with all submission requirements.

Why This Consultancy Matters

This is more than just a consulting contract—it is an opportunity to:

  • Influence global migration policy and humanitarian strategies
  • Work with a leading international NGO
  • Contribute to research that impacts millions of displaced people
  • Strengthen your professional portfolio in international development and migration studies

For researchers, policy experts, and consultants specializing in migration, this is a high-value opportunity to engage in meaningful, real-world impact.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Research on Regular & Irregular Migration with Danish Refugee Council (DRC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Knight Cities Challenge 2026: Win Up to $200,000 to Transform Your Community with a Bold Idea
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Knight Cities Challenge 2026 is officially open, offering a powerful opportunity for innovators, community leaders, creatives, and organizations to bring impactful ideas to life. Backed by the Knight Foundation, this $5 million funding initiative supports community-driven solutions designed to create lasting change across selected cities. If you have a bold, innovative idea that can […]

The post Knight Cities Challenge 2026: Win Up to $200,000 to Transform Your Community with a Bold Idea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Knight Cities Challenge 2026 is officially open, offering a powerful opportunity for innovators, community leaders, creatives, and organizations to bring impactful ideas to life. Backed by the Knight Foundation, this $5 million funding initiative supports community-driven solutions designed to create lasting change across selected cities.

If you have a bold, innovative idea that can strengthen your community, improve local systems, or foster meaningful connections, this challenge is your chance to secure up to $200,000 in funding and make it happen.

What is the Knight Cities Challenge?

The Knight Cities Challenge (KCC) is an open grant competition aimed at democratizing access to funding by inviting anyone with a strong idea—individuals, nonprofits, and organizations—to apply.

This initiative builds on years of successful open innovation programs led by the Knight Foundation, focusing on empowering local communities to solve their own challenges through creativity and collaboration.

For 2026, the program will fund a new cohort of projects that are:

  • Community-centered
  • Innovative and scalable
  • Designed to create long-term local impact

Funding Details and Project Scope

Selected applicants will receive:

  • 💰 Up to $200,000 in grant funding per project
  • 🗓️ Project timeline: Must be completed within one year
  • 🌍 Access to a network of changemakers and support from Knight Foundation

The total funding pool for this cycle is $5 million, making it one of the most competitive and impactful community-focused grant programs globally.

Focus Areas for 2026

All project ideas must align with at least one of the following priority areas:

1. Strengthening Local News and Information

Projects that improve access to reliable, community-centered information and local journalism.

2. Expanding Economic Opportunity

Innovative ideas that create jobs, support entrepreneurship, or open new pathways for economic mobility.

3. Building Community Through Arts and Culture

Projects that bring people together through creative expression, public spaces, and cultural initiatives.

Who Can Apply?

The Knight Cities Challenge is uniquely inclusive and flexible:

  • Open to individuals, nonprofits, and organizations
  • No requirement to be a previous grant recipient
  • Applicants do not need to be a nonprofit
  • Collaborative and partnership-based ideas are encouraged

However, projects must benefit one of Knight Foundation’s 26 communities, which include cities like:

  • Akron, Ohio
  • Detroit, Michigan
  • Miami, Florida
  • Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
  • San Jose, California
  • Charlotte, North Carolina
  • Boulder, Colorado
  • And more

Application Process

Applying is straightforward and designed to encourage participation:

  • Submit your application online through the official Knight Cities Challenge platform
  • Clearly explain your idea, its impact, and how it aligns with one of the focus areas
  • Provide a realistic plan for execution within one year

Additional Support for Applicants:

  • 📺 Informational webinars with Q&A sessions
  • 📅 Office hours with Knight Foundation staff (first-come, first-served)
  • 📩 Email updates with tips and reminders

Important Deadline

  • Application Opens: April 1, 2026
  • Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)

Late submissions will not be accepted, so early application is highly recommended.

Why You Should Apply

The Knight Cities Challenge is more than just a grant—it’s a platform for changemakers to:

  • Turn bold ideas into real-world solutions
  • Gain visibility and credibility
  • Access funding without traditional barriers
  • Contribute to meaningful, community-driven change

Whether your idea involves media innovation, economic empowerment, or cultural engagement, this challenge is designed to help you bring it to life.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Knight Cities Challenge 2026: Win Up to $200,000 to Transform Your Community with a Bold Idea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICCR Scholarship Scheme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Study Opportunities in India for African Students
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The ICCR Scholarship Scheme for the Academic Year 2026–2027 is a prestigious initiative launched by the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR), under the Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India. This scholarship program represents a significant opportunity for students especially from Africa to pursue fully funded higher education in India across a wide range […]

The post ICCR Scholarship Scheme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Study Opportunities in India for African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ICCR Scholarship Scheme for the Academic Year 2026–2027 is a prestigious initiative launched by the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR), under the Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India. This scholarship program represents a significant opportunity for students especially from Africa to pursue fully funded higher education in India across a wide range of academic and cultural disciplines.

With access to 131 top Indian universities and institutes, this program is designed to foster international academic collaboration, strengthen cultural ties, and empower students to achieve global academic excellence.

Overview of the ICCR Scholarship Scheme 2026–2027

The ICCR scholarship offers multiple schemes tailored to different academic and cultural interests. For the 2026–2027 academic year, two major scholarship tracks are available:

1. India–Africa Maitri Scholarship Scheme

This scheme is specifically designed to strengthen India-Africa relations by supporting African students in pursuing higher education in India.

  • Available Slots (Example: Ghana): 70
  • Programs Offered:
    • Undergraduate (UG)
    • Postgraduate (PG)
    • PhD
  • Fields of Study:
    • STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics)
    • Non-STEM disciplines
  • Excluded Fields:
    • Medical and paramedical courses
    • Law
    • Integrated degree programs

2. Lata Mangeshkar Dance & Music Scholarship Scheme

This globally accessible scholarship focuses on preserving and promoting Indian cultural heritage.

  • Total Slots: 100 globally
    • 75 slots for cultural studies (Dance, Music, Theatre, Sculpture, Indian Languages, Cuisine, etc.)
    • 25 slots for a 1-year Diploma in Yoga at specialized institutions in India
  • Institutions for Yoga Diploma:
    • Dev Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya (Uttarakhand)
    • Kaivalyadhama Yoga Institute (Maharashtra)
    • Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga (New Delhi)
    • Swami Vivekananda Yoga Anusandhana Samsthana (Bangalore)
  • Programs Offered:
    • UG, PG, PhD (Cultural disciplines)
    • Diploma (Yoga)
  • Allocation Rule: First-come, first-served basis with country-specific limits

Financial Coverage and Benefits

The ICCR Scholarship is fully funded, offering comprehensive financial support to selected candidates:

  • Return economy class airfare (to India and back)
  • Full tuition fee coverage
  • Monthly stipend (living allowance)
  • Accommodation (campus hostel or designated housing)
  • Contingency and thesis/dissertation allowances
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA) where applicable

This support structure ensures that students can focus entirely on their academic journey without financial stress.

Costs Not Covered by the Scholarship

While the scholarship is generous, students are expected to cover certain expenses:

  • Hostel or private accommodation charges (in some cases)
  • Food and mess charges
  • Security deposits and caution money
  • Registration fees and penalties
  • Certification and documentation fees
  • Immigration-related costs

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the ICCR Scholarship Scheme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must be a national of an eligible country (e.g., Ghana or other African nations depending on scheme)
  • Must hold a valid passport with at least 2 years validity as of 1 July 2026
  • Minimum age: 18 years
  • Maximum age:
    • UG/PG/Diploma: 35 years
    • PhD: 40 years
  • English proficiency required (essay or TOEFL/IELTS accepted)
  • Academic prerequisites depending on chosen course (e.g., science students must have relevant subjects in high school)

Important Academic Restrictions

Applicants should carefully note the following:

  • No admission to B.Tech programs in IITs
  • No scholarships for:
    • Medical or paramedical fields (e.g., Nursing, Physiotherapy)
    • Integrated degrees (e.g., BALLB, combined BSc/MSc)
  • Specialized courses like Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, etc., fall under separate AYUSH scholarships

Medical Fitness Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be in good physical and mental health
  • Provide a certified medical fitness report
  • Undergo additional health screening upon arrival in India

Failure to meet medical standards may result in cancellation and repatriation at the student’s expense.

Important Dates

  • Application Portal Opens: 27 February 2026
  • Application Deadline: 15 April 2026
  • University Admission Decisions: 30 April 2026
  • Scholarship Allocation Deadline: 15 May 2026
  • Acceptance Deadline: 15 May 2026

Why You Should Apply

The ICCR Scholarship Scheme is more than just financial aid—it is a gateway to:

  • International academic exposure
  • Cultural exchange and global networking
  • High-quality education in one of the world’s fastest-growing economies
  • Career advancement opportunities across multiple sectors

For students aiming to build a global career while experiencing India’s rich academic and cultural landscape, this scholarship is a life-changing opportunity.

For more information click here 

APPPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post ICCR Scholarship Scheme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Study Opportunities in India for African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026: Empowering One Million Young African Leaders to Shape the Future by 2030
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026 is a transformative leadership initiative designed to identify, equip, and connect passionate young Africans who are committed to driving sustainable development and socioeconomic transformation across the continent. Organized under the One Million Leaders Africa (OMLA) movement, this fellowship is part of a bold vision to raise a network of one […]

The post OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026: Empowering One Million Young African Leaders to Shape the Future by 2030 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026 is a transformative leadership initiative designed to identify, equip, and connect passionate young Africans who are committed to driving sustainable development and socioeconomic transformation across the continent. Organized under the One Million Leaders Africa (OMLA) movement, this fellowship is part of a bold vision to raise a network of one million purpose-driven leaders who will actively contribute to shaping Africa’s future by the year 2030.

With a growing community of over 4,000 young leaders engaged since its inception in 2020, the OMLA Fellowship stands as a powerful platform for leadership development, mentorship, and community impact. The programme is targeted at innovative and purpose-driven individuals who are ready to lead change, inspire others, and create lasting impact within their communities and beyond.

About the OMLA Fellowship Programme

The OMLA Fellowship is more than just a training programme—it is a movement. It is built on the belief that Africa’s transformation lies in the hands of young leaders who are equipped with the right mindset, skills, and networks.

Vision of the Programme

The fellowship is aligned with OMLA’s overarching goal:

  • To build a network of 1 million young African leaders by 2030
  • To empower youth to lead initiatives that drive sustainable development
  • To foster community-based leadership and mentorship
  • To create a ripple effect where each fellow mentors others within their ecosystem

What the Fellowship Offers

Participants in the OMLA Fellowship Programme gain access to a wide range of personal and professional development opportunities.

1. Leadership Development

  • Structured leadership training sessions
  • Exposure to real-world problem-solving frameworks
  • Development of critical thinking and decision-making skills

2. Mentorship and Community Impact

  • Fellows are encouraged to mentor others in their communities
  • Opportunity to impact dozens of individuals through knowledge transfer
  • Engagement in community-based projects and initiatives

3. Network and Collaboration

  • Access to a growing network of over 4,000 young African leaders
  • Opportunities for collaboration across countries and sectors
  • Participation in OMLA Clubs and alumni networks

4. Access to Resources

  • Fellowship Information Package outlining programme structure
  • Learning materials and leadership tools
  • Continuous updates and communication from the OMLA Secretariat

5. Platform for Visibility

  • Opportunity to be featured among OMLA Fellows
  • Participation in events, summits, and bootcamps
  • Engagement with like-minded changemakers across Africa

Fellowship Structure and Engagement

The OMLA Fellowship Programme is designed to provide a holistic leadership journey that combines learning, mentorship, and action.

Key Components Include:

  1. Fellowship Training
    • Focus on leadership, innovation, and sustainability
  2. Community Engagement
    • Fellows implement impact-driven initiatives
  3. Mentorship Chain Model
    • Each fellow mentors others, creating a multiplier effect
  4. Alumni Engagement
    • Continued support through alumni networks and programmes

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is open to young Africans who demonstrate passion, commitment, and leadership potential.

Applicants Must:

  • Be a young African with a strong desire to create impact
  • Demonstrate passion for leadership and community development
  • Show evidence of innovation, creativity, or initiative
  • Be willing to actively participate in the fellowship activities
  • Commit to mentoring others as part of the programme

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

To apply for the OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026, applicants must follow a structured process:

Step 1: Download the Information Package

  • Carefully review the fellowship details
  • Understand expectations, structure, and selection criteria

Step 2: Verify Your Email Address

  • Complete the email verification form
  • Confirm your interest in applying

Step 3: Complete the Application Form

  • Receive a secure application link via email
  • Fill out and submit your application

Step 4: Monitor Your Email

  • Check for updates on your application status
  • Ensure you also check your spam/junk folder

Why You Should Apply

The OMLA Fellowship is ideal for young leaders who are ready to step into positions of influence and drive change.

Key Reasons to Apply:

  • Become part of a continental movement of changemakers
  • Gain leadership skills that are applicable across sectors
  • Build a strong network of African leaders
  • Contribute to Africa’s development agenda
  • Access mentorship and growth opportunities

Programme Impact So Far

  • Over 4,000 young leaders engaged since 2020
  • Fellows actively mentoring others in their communities
  • Growing presence across multiple African countries

Important Information

  • Application Deadline: Not explicitly stated (apply early)
  • Location: Open to applicants across Africa
  • Mode: Fellowship with community engagement components

Contact Information

  • Email:
    • olla@nelisglobal.org
    • onemillionleadersafrica@gmail.com
  • Location:
    KM 6, Igbatoro Road, Akure, Ondo State, Nigeria
  • Phone:
    +2349136552102

Click HERE to learn more about the OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship and African opportunities on OFY.

The post OMLA Fellowship Programme 2026: Empowering One Million Young African Leaders to Shape the Future by 2030 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
